# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark construction details, quantitative task-resolution results, cost and behavior effects of context files, and bounded evidence about when context files help or fail for coding agents.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: result
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, AGENTbench overview
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
AGENTbench contains 138 task instances drawn from 12 repositories that include developer-written context files.

## Source Excerpt

"The benchmark contains 138 138 unique instances, covering both bug-fixing and feature addition tasks across 12 12 recent and niche repositories, which all feature developer-written context files."

## Note

Dataset composition statement from the paper's benchmark description.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Developer-provided context files increased performance by 4% on average relative to omitting context files.

## Source Excerpt

"Surprisingly, we observe that developer-provided files only marginally improve performance compared to omitting them entirely (an increase of 4% on average)"

## Note

Average improvement claim is stated in the introduction summary.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
LLM-generated context files decreased agent performance by 3% on average.

## Source Excerpt

"while LLM-generated context files have a small negative effect on agent performance (a decrease of 3% on average)"

## Note

This conflicts with the common recommendation to add generated context files.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Context files increased agent costs by over 20%.

## Source Excerpt

"context files lead to increased exploration, testing, and reasoning by coding agents, and, as a result, increase costs by over 20%."

## Note

This is a top-level cost effect reported in the introduction.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The generated tests for AGENTbench achieved 75% average coverage of the modified code.

## Source Excerpt

"The resulting tests achieve an average coverage of 75% of the modified code (see Table ˜ 1 )."

## Note

This is a benchmark-construction quality metric for the evaluation test sets.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: result
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
LLM-generated context files reduced performance in 5 of 8 evaluated settings across SWE-bench Lite and AGENTbench.

## Source Excerpt

"LLM-generated context files cause performance drops in 5 out of 8 settings across SWE-bench Lite and AGENTbench"

## Note

This introduces tension with recommendations that generated context files broadly help coding agents.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
With LLM-generated context files, average resolution rate fell by 0.5% on SWE-bench Lite and by 2% on AGENTbench.

## Source Excerpt

"In more detail, the average resolution rate is reduced by 0.5 % 0.5\% and 2 % 2\% on average on SWE-bench Lite and AGENTbench , respectively."

## Note

This is the benchmark-specific breakdown of the average performance drop.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
LLM-generated context files increased average step counts by 2.45 on SWE-bench Lite and 3.92 on AGENTbench.

## Source Excerpt

"the context files increase the # steps in every setting on average by 2.45 2.45 and 3.92 3.92 steps, respectively"

## Note

This is behavioral evidence about added process overhead, not direct evidence of why higher step counts improve or harm task success.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
LLM-generated context files increased average costs by 20% on SWE-bench Lite and 23% on AGENTbench.

## Source Excerpt

"which leads to a cost increase of 20% and 23% on average, respectively"

## Note

This is the benchmark-specific cost breakdown paired with the step-count increase.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: result
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Developer-provided context files outperformed LLM-generated context files for all four evaluated agents.

## Source Excerpt

"We observe that the developer-provided context files outperform the LLM-generated ones for all four agents"

## Note

This comparison is within the evaluated agent set in the paper.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Developer-provided context files increased average step counts by 3.34 and increased costs by as much as 19%.

## Source Excerpt

"However, developer-provided context files also increase the average number of steps and costs required to solve the task, on average by 3.34 3.34 steps and at most 19%, respectively."

## Note

This is the paper's reported overhead for human-written context files.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: claim-support
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Context files did not meaningfully reduce the number of steps before agents first touched files modified in the original PR patch.

## Source Excerpt

"Both on SWE-bench Lite and AGENTbench the presence of context files does not meaningfully reduce this metric, as shown in Figure ˜ 4 ."

## Note

This is evidence against the claimed repository-overview benefit, but it supports that conclusion through one operational proxy rather than a direct causal test.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: metric
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
When a context file mentioned uv, agents used uv 1.6 times per instance on average, versus fewer than 0.01 times when uv was not mentioned.

## Source Excerpt

"For instance, uv is used 1.6 times per instance on average when mentioned in the context files, compared to fewer than 0.01 times when it is not mentioned"

## Note

This is bounded mechanism evidence showing instruction-following behavior for a named tool.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-001
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Gloaguen et al., 2026, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11988
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
When other repository documentation was removed, LLM-generated context files improved performance by 2.7% on average and outperformed developer-written documentation.

## Source Excerpt

"In this setting, where context files are the only source of documentation available, LLM-generated context files not only consistently improve performance by 2.7 2.7 % on average, but also outperform developer-written documentation."

## Note

This is a conditional result from an altered documentation setting, so it should not be read as the paper's main result for normal repository conditions.
---
